channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Bumblebees have tiny brains but they can solve problems like chimps and elephants - NPR
item.description:
1. Bumblebees have tiny brains but they can solve problems like chimps and elephants  NPR
  2. Are bumblebees as smart as chimps? A new problem-solving experiment makes the case  CNN
  3. ‘They surprise me every time’: bees can use tools to solve problems, study finds  The Guardian
  4. Bumble bees show spontaneous problem-solving, challenging big-brain assumptions  Phys.org
  5. Bumblebees use tools to solve complex problems—despite not being trained to do so  Scientific American

item.description:
1. Northern Lights Forecast: Aurora Could Be Visible In These 9 States Sunday  Forbes

  4. NASA concerns about Russian repairs prompted ISS safe haven decision  SpaceNews
  5. NASA reverses evacuation alert order for astronauts aboard space station  Reuters

item.title: Largest scorpion ever grew to over three-feet long and had six-inch pinchers, according to nightmare-inducing study - New York Post
item.description:
1. Largest scorpion ever grew to over three-feet long and had six-inch pinchers, according to nightmare-inducing study  New York Post

  3. World’s biggest scorpions were the size of baseball bats  Yahoo
  4. Giant Scorpions Ruled Ancient Britain during Devonian Period  Sci.News
  5. World's largest scorpion roamed UK 400 million years ago, says expert  BBC

item.title: Scientists have made a huge discovery at the enormous black hole at the centre of our Galaxy. And they found something pointing right at it - BBC Sky at Night Magazine
item.description:
1. Scientists have made a huge discovery at the enormous black hole at the centre of our Galaxy. And they found something pointing right at it  BBC Sky at Night Magazine
  2. Wind from Milky Way's supermassive black hole is finally discovered  Reuters
  3. Even quiet black holes create winds, new Milky Way observations reveal  Science News
  4. Astronomers just solved a 50-year-old mystery about the Milky Way’s black hole  Scientific American
  5. They've Been Searching for the Milky Way's Black Hole Wind for 50 Years and Finally Found It  Universe Today

item.title: Ancient Chinese medicine could transform hair loss treatment - ScienceDaily
item.description: Ancient Chinese medicine could transform hair loss treatment  ScienceDaily

item.title: The largest insects that ever lived were dragonflies with wingspans of more than two feet, grown in an ancient atmosphere so much richer in oxygen that nothing that size could survive in the air we breathe today - Space Daily
item.description: The largest insects that ever lived were dragonflies with wingspans of more than two feet, grown in an ancient atmosphere so much richer in oxygen that nothing that size could survive in the air we breathe today  Space Daily

item.title: NASA's James Webb Discovers That 3I/ATLAS Let One Rip as It Passed Through Solar System - Futurism
item.description:
1. NASA's James Webb Discovers That 3I/ATLAS Let One Rip as It Passed Through Solar System  Futurism
  2. NASA’s Webb Detects Methane on Interstellar Comet 3I/ATLAS  NASA Science (.gov)
  3. Webb Telescope snaps interstellar comet – proves it's like nothing we've ever seen in our own Solar System  BBC Sky at Night Magazine
  4. NASA's Webb detects methane and strange chemistry on interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS  ScienceDaily
  5. REAL STARDUST? 🌠 The European Space Agency has shared the first-ever chemical fingerprint it captured when the interstellar Comet 3I/ATLAS exited the solar system after swinging around the Sun. (📷: European Space Agency/IG)  facebook.com

item.title: Scientists Find Chunk of Lost Planet in Desert - Yahoo
item.description: Scientists Find Chunk of Lost Planet in Desert  YahooSee more headlines & perspectives on Google News

item.title: There are millions of objects in our own solar system that have never been seen and the facility that will find them is now just months from starting its decade-long survey - Space Daily
item.description: There are millions of objects in our own solar system that have never been seen and the facility that will find them is now just months from starting its decade-long survey  Space Daily

item.title: In 1959, the Soviet Luna 3 probe swung around the far side of the Moon and took 29 grainy photographs of a hemisphere humans had never seen, then developed the film onboard and scanned the negatives with a flying-spot beam to radio them back across - Space Daily
item.description: In 1959, the Soviet Luna 3 probe swung around the far side of the Moon and took 29 grainy photographs of a hemisphere humans had never seen, then developed the film onboard and scanned the negatives with a flying-spot beam to radio them back across  Space Daily

item.title: Mount Etna eruptions reveal carbon dioxide and water can trigger separate explosive paths - Phys.org
item.description: Mount Etna eruptions reveal carbon dioxide and water can trigger separate explosive paths  Phys.org

item.title: Parker Solar Probe has flown through the Sun’s outer atmosphere, the corona, where temperatures soar into the millions of degrees even though the visible surface below is far cooler — one of the strangest long-running puzzles in solar physics. - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Parker Solar Probe has flown through the Sun’s outer atmosphere, the corona, where temperatures soar into the millions of degrees even though the visible surface below is far cooler — one of the strangest long-running puzzles in solar physics.  Space Daily
  2. NASA’s Parker Solar Probe is flying at 430,000 mph through the Sun’s atmosphere, and its 4.5-inch shield is why it survives  OkDiario
  3. Red-hot voyage to sun will bring us closer to our star  Nine.com.au

item.title: The International Space Station circles the planet so fast that the crew watch the sun rise and set sixteen times in a single day, a new dawn roughly every ninety minutes - Space Daily
item.description: The International Space Station circles the planet so fast that the crew watch the sun rise and set sixteen times in a single day, a new dawn roughly every ninety minutes  Space Daily

item.title: Why the years seem to speed up as we age, and how focusing on one thing might make time feel slower again - Space Daily
item.description: Why the years seem to speed up as we age, and how focusing on one thing might make time feel slower again  Space DailySee more headlines & perspectives on Google News

item.title: Chemists have demonstrated for the first time how RNA may have copied itself on early Earth — solving a bottleneck that had blocked the origin-of-life field for decades - Space Daily
item.description: Chemists have demonstrated for the first time how RNA may have copied itself on early Earth — solving a bottleneck that had blocked the origin-of-life field for decades  Space Daily

item.title: Scientists Discover Hidden Symmetry on Earth That Nobody Can Explain - 404 Media
item.description:
1. Scientists Discover Hidden Symmetry on Earth That Nobody Can Explain  404 Media
  2. Earth’s east–west albedo symmetry  Nature
  3. Previously Hidden Line Of Symmetry Splits Earth Into Two Halves That Equally Reflect Solar Radiation  IFLScience
  4. Earth has a mysterious triple symmetry that may influence its climate  New Scientist
  5. Understanding Earth's hidden east-west symmetry could improve climate models  Phys.org

item.title: Predictive surrogates could cut quantum computing measurement overhead by more than 99.97% - Phys.org
item.description: Predictive surrogates could cut quantum computing measurement overhead by more than 99.97%  Phys.org

  4. Indian “Rocket Woman” sari displayed at US Space Museum  Minute Mirror
  5. A sari for Mars: Outfit worn by Indian 'rocket woman' at US museum  AOL.com

item.title: More than 65% of all the fresh water on Earth sits in one part of the world — and it's almost entirely beyond our reach - Space Daily
item.description: More than 65% of all the fresh water on Earth sits in one part of the world — and it's almost entirely beyond our reach  Space Daily

item.title: Scientists have discovered that air filters can capture DNA floating in the atmosphere and it can identify wildlife, viruses, and even plants linked to drugs nearby - Space Daily
item.description: Scientists have discovered that air filters can capture DNA floating in the atmosphere and it can identify wildlife, viruses, and even plants linked to drugs nearby  Space Daily

item.title: I resisted smart telescopes for years — then one changed my life - Space
item.description: I resisted smart telescopes for years — then one changed my life  Space

item.title: An AI trawled through 35 years of Hubble images and found more than 800 strange objects that had never been documented before, showing that one of astronomy’s most famous archives is still hiding discoveries in plain sight - Space Daily
item.description: An AI trawled through 35 years of Hubble images and found more than 800 strange objects that had never been documented before, showing that one of astronomy’s most famous archives is still hiding discoveries in plain sight  Space Daily

item.title: Why can't we figure out how strong gravity is? - Live Science
item.description: Why can't we figure out how strong gravity is?  Live Science

item.title: A 2013 University of Michigan study on rats found that in the 30 seconds after cardiac arrest, the brain produced a surge of high-frequency gamma waves more coherent than during waking life, a neural fireworks display some researchers think could underlie t - Space Daily
item.description: A 2013 University of Michigan study on rats found that in the 30 seconds after cardiac arrest, the brain produced a surge of high-frequency gamma waves more coherent than during waking life, a neural fireworks display some researchers think could underlie t  Space Daily

item.title: The James Webb telescope keeps finding early galaxies that look brighter, bigger and more mature than astronomers expected, forcing researchers to rethink how quickly the first galaxies formed stars and assembled after the Big Bang. - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The James Webb telescope keeps finding early galaxies that look brighter, bigger and more mature than astronomers expected, forcing researchers to rethink how quickly the first galaxies formed stars and assembled after the Big Bang.  Space Daily
  2. 'Astonishing': James Webb telescope spots the most chemically primitive galaxy in the ancient universe  Live Science
  3. The Galaxy That Forgot to Spin  Universe Today

item.title: The Moons of Uranus May Hold the Key to Finding Missing Planets - WIRED
item.description:
1. The Moons of Uranus May Hold the Key to Finding Missing Planets  WIRED
  2. The Solar System May Have Lost 2 Planets, And The Mystery Just Deepened  Yahoo
  3. A 'lost planet' may have given Jupiter and Uranus their moons  Space
  4. One of our planets may be missing, and it could explain why the solar system looks the way it does  Phys.org
  5. Our Solar System May Once Have Had Five Giant Planets – And That Could Help Solve Some Solar System Mysteries  IFLScience
